gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/65)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/65)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) كُونُوا دُعَاةً لِلنَّاسِ بِغَيْرِ أَلْسِنَتِكُمْ لِيَرَوْا مِنْكُمُ الْوَرَعَ وَ الِاجْتِهَادَ وَ الصَّلَاةَ وَ الْخَيْرَ فَإِنَّ ذَلِكَ دَاعِيَةٌ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hajjal, from Al A’ala, from Ibn Abu Yafour who said,
‘Abu Abdullahasws said: ‘Become inviting to the people without (using) your tongues. Let them see from you the devoutness, and the striving, and the Salāt, and the goodness, for that is the inviter’.
en.sarwar: 14. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Hajjal from al-‘Ala’ from ibn abu Ya’fur from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) has said, ‘Preach to people without your tongue. You must show them restraint from worldly matters (sins). You should perform hard work, prayer and good deeds. Such behavior includes preaching.”
